name: Rich
what_it_does: Python library for rich text and beautiful terminal formatting. Renders colored text, tables, progress bars, markdown, JSON, syntax-highlighted code, tree structures, and live displays in the terminal. Used for building polished CLI agent interfaces, debug output, and monitoring dashboards in the terminal.
use_cases: Display agent progress with Rich progress bars and spinners during long-running LLM or data processing tasks
Format agent debug output with syntax-highlighted JSON, tables, and colored log levels for readability
Build polished agent CLI interfaces with Rich's Markdown rendering, tables, and styled output
Log structured agent data (tool calls, responses, metrics) with Rich's logging handler for colored console output
Create agent monitoring dashboards in the terminal using Rich's Live and Layout components
not_for: Web UI development — Rich is terminal-only; use Streamlit or Gradio for web-based agent UIs
Production logging in JSON format — Rich is for human-readable terminal output; use structlog or logging with JSON handlers for machine-readable logs
Environments without ANSI terminal support (some CI systems, Windows cmd) — check terminal capabilities
best_when: You're building a Python CLI agent and want beautiful, readable terminal output with colors, tables, and progress indicators without wrestling with ANSI escape codes.
avoid_when: You need machine-readable output for log aggregation — Rich's colored output is not JSON-parseable; use structlog for machine-readable agent logs.

auth.notes: Local output formatting library — no authentication required.

pricing.notes: MIT license. Created by Will McGugan (Textualize). Also used as the display backend for Textual (TUI framework).

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — local output library. Performance is terminal I/O bound.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Rich itself has excellent error display with traceback formatting (rich.traceback.install()). Library errors are rare and clear. Markup validation errors indicate the invalid Rich markup syntax.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Output formatting library — pure output with no side effects on data.
known_agent_gotchas: ANSI color codes may not render in all environments — CI pipelines (GitHub Actions, Jenkins) may or may not support ANSI; use FORCE_COLOR=1 or NO_COLOR=1 to control color output explicitly
Rich markup uses [bold], [red], etc. syntax which conflicts with f-string formatting — escape brackets with double [[ ]] in dynamically generated strings to prevent markup injection
progress.track() is a generator that must be consumed — forgetting to iterate the result doesn't display progress; always use in a for loop
Rich Live with multiple renderables requires Layout — ad-hoc print() inside a Live context corrupts the display; use live.update() for all updates within a Live context
Console.print() and regular print() can interfere when both are used — pick one style for consistent output; mixing causes duplicate newlines and layout issues
Rich tables with dynamic content: add_row() must be called before rendering; updating table cells after rendering requires reconstructing the table for Live displays

security_score_components.security_notes: Output formatting library. No network calls. Avoid using Rich.print() to display untrusted data with Rich markup — user input could inject markup. Use escape() or no markup for untrusted strings.
